inline_comments.message:
As per sonar it is important that is exception is thrown:
https://sonarcloud.io/organizations/onap/rules?open=java%3AS2142&rule_key=java%3AS2142
Unit tests should be fast and run often. Spending 1 min for 1 test is not ideal.
I have removed this. see latest patch
length of time needs to be decided
This method allows a session to try get an anchor lock and will throw exception if it has to wait as another session is holding the lock
consider making it configurable... I can talk to you about some approaches...

i think the whole concept of have a method that wait on  a  lock is out of the scope of this user story, I actually think it is client responsibility to wait for a lock or not. I will download the patch now and have a close look

extract out constant DEFAULT_LOCK_ATTEMPT_TIMEOUT = 10 (that's enough!)
overload lockAnchor method (in Java service API) with an optional timeout in ms

delegate to session manager

lockAnchor(sesiobId, EntytiClass, entityId)
clarify by extracting small method: releaseLock(session)
clarify by extracting small method: resumeSession(session)
undo any blank space changes (use history)
might not be needed anymore once this class takes responsibility for session stuff

suggest tests
1) just get a lock (nothing to asserts)
2) just unlock
3) attemt to lock with invalid session id
3a) lock by other session will fail
3b) lock by other session will succeed after unlock
4) lock by same session ?
tets in session manager spec instead (some of it wil remain here but just proof it is delegated with correct parameters)
insert space and just say "Unlock Anchor (commit changes)"
Exact, this was an old comment ;)
"Retrying the sending of the request could be required." might be better
releaseLocksOnAnchor to match lock method naming

Or to above: might be better to remove 'Anchor' from logging and then can be used for all locks
Attempting to unlock entities and Entities successfully unlocked possibly

I think for these tests the sessionManager should be mocked because otherwise its the same tests as those that are in the sessionManager groovy testing. This should check the correct sessionManager method is being called. Would also remove the need for the Sql annotation at the start of the lock test
Does this need its own serialized version id?
Same as before, either 'unlock entities method is called' or rename function to releaseAnchorLocks
Remove if this is ready to be added
Remove this file if there are no changes
As this is being used only once it can be inlined. The name does give context but from the api it can be seen that it is the default timeout.

As per sections 4.2 and 4.3 in the article above, the interrupted status needs to be restored by calling `interrupt()` method: https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/11/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Thread.html#interrupt()
Is this required for this change ? What is the reason ?

The difference between releaseLocks() here and existing closeSession() is not so clear from an interface point of view.
The implementation needs to be read to understand that both methods are committing the transaction and then one is closing the session and the other one is starting a new transaction using the same session.

What is the reason and the use case to start a new transaction on the same session ? What does it bring compare to starting a new transaction using the existing startSession() method ?
It would be good to add the actual exception as the exception cause of this one to ensure that no information is left under the way.
As there is some logs here, I'm suggesting logging seesion id and anchor id to make the trace more meaningful and useful.

Is it required and a good idea to let the client specify whatever timeout he wishes ?
The client would be able to get and keep the connections for any amount of time he decides, preventing other requests to get some connections.
Any reason why this exception is not extending a type from CpsException hierarchy ?
it would be up to the client and letting the client specify the timeout allows it to be configurable
No reason, should it extend CpsException?
should I specify it to be 'default' when the other test method is running with the default timeout? this scenario is for passing a custom timeout

this issue was discussed and Lathish pushed a quick fix which has since been merged, https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/128492
so rebase should clear this too: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/cps/+/128492
This is already in master. I think if you do a rebase with master it should pick this up :)
I'm confused as to why changes are being picked up in this file?
Is it necessary for this to be a completely new class, as opposed to just a single method within the session manager?
to close the session is called
allows for mocking on the groovy test
it is no longer a POC! Also maybe add a bit more detail
NB: you haven't modified this file
move this into the sessioNManager method
this is your 'cause' see comments on your SessionTimeoutException constructor!
I think this method should take in a anchor name an look up the id itself...
can the logger itself not do the String formatting?!
I think this should be debug level logging
add id (or name as per previous comment on method parameter)

debug level 
(we probably need some guidelines on what level to use when it is always a contentious subject and hard to get right)

typically no need to mock ObjectMapper, use real thing instead
or if not used at all use a Stub

(unless testing error scenarios but even then a Spy might be better)
I think these test are now redundant, you have 'delegate' test in CpsDataServiceImplSpec.groovy and spearte intergation test on the sesionmanager itself

I don't think you need to to this in test, since each test wil run its own clean environment
If need use cleanup() or cleanupSpec() fixture methods ,see https://spockframework.org/spock/docs/1.0/spock_primer.html

better declare these once as common fields
(can override in specific tets where needed)
see previous comment about the need for this
a session manager exception is thrown, saying it was interrupted
a session manager exception is thrown, saying it was aborted
a session manager exception is thrown, mentioning both sessions

do not use a dummy exception as cause, only use caught exceptions as 'cause'
I think you can just use a constructor without cause here
it was modified for the update of the version but this was not edited
i have also tried removing it from commit but it still came up

releasing locks will only close and commit the changes within the transaction, it will then start a new transaction for further operations

while close session will only commit and close session

see latest patch
this file was not changed on this commit 😕

WIP
compare to previous patch (patch 16) 

since the move of repository class methods to sessionManager class- this test class had to be refactored to use @Autowired for other test cases

this test case seems to fail and ignores the interruptedException thrown under the 'given'

Commit message does not follow onap guidelines:
https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Commit+Messages#CommitMessages-CommitStructure
(one blank line between summary and body)

I think that the interface should not be over complicated if not required. 
Why not removing releaseLocks() and only provide the close() method ? The client is still able to start a new session if needed. Do I miss something ...

The cause of the actual exception is not the exception to be thrown.

I presume that the intent here is to throw a SessionManagerException (same as the other situations).
Agreed during the daily that wrapping this one into SessionManagerException will be done in a separate commit under the same Jira.
Yes, it should be part of the hierarchy somewhere, for consistency and to facilitate exception handling and mapping in the rest layer.
removed as agreed in daily meeting. see latest patch
suggestion: would be good to add (using a second then) to show when eeh first session is closed the other sesion can acquire the lock
this file has not been modified
If only used once, they can be inlined below
is this detail needed in the javadocs?
These can be inlined or if same as lines 448 - 450 then they can be scoped at class level and used as constants
Yes I believe so, to give more details in unlocking anchor as there is NO method to unlock anchor but to close the current session holding it
In newSingleThreadExecutor the threads are not reused but with newfixedthreadpool we can reuse them.
we can use executor.submit() which returns future and pass the timeout to the future to get the result which is quite handy rather than introducing whole new class for timelimit.
I guess this wont be needed if we use threadpool
will this be necessary if I only need one to run the timelimiter ?
new class for timelimit was added to create unit test

Tried this and it seems that hibernate creates a new object instead of using the same entities therefore, I get the hibernate error 'a different object with the same identifier value was already associated with the session'

tested it aswell with locking the same anchor twice with the same session and it fails
query: why do we need 2 different methods with exactly the same implementation? What is the relevance of custom timeout?

As per the Jira, the client should be able to set this... so do we want to set this in a properties file ?
And define a default value as well, thus we could remove overloading lockAnchor();
see comment on sessionManager class about default timeout
this is why there is 2 different methods on the service layer which allows the client to configure it , if they don't give a custom timeout then the default will be used
I am not sure it matters much. See what the documentation sasy. we can always change later if needed
NB: as you are testing parameter passing I would tets with a more unique value like 1234L

NB: I actually thought it was good to have that knowledge documented in the (at least production) code!
best case I would even implement minimum timeout in the production code :-) But don't to that now, let's get this merged!
this is the same value as the timeout should be different to proof it is NOT teh default used on line 448
